---
title: "Data Science and Strategy for Business"
subtitle: "ER114"
author: "Prof. Dr. Jörg Schoder"
institute: "FFHS" 
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
bibliography: ../../lit/my_bib.bib
lang: en-US
reference-section-title: References
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    self_contained: true
    css: 
         - default
         - ../../css/ffhs-theme_js.css
         - xaringan-themer.css
    includes:
      after_body: ../../css/insert-logo.html
    lib_dir: ../../libs
    nature:
      slideNumberFormat: "%current%/%total%"
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
    seal: false
    

    
---
class: title-slide

```{r xaringan-themer, include=FALSE}
library(xaringanthemer)
style_xaringan(text_color = "#d50006",inverse_text_color = "#FFFFFF",inverse_background_color = "#d50006", title_slide_background_color = "#d50006",header_background_color = "#d50006",header_color = "#FFFFFF",header_h1_font_size = "32px",
  header_h2_font_size = "26px",link_color="#502479",
  header_h3_font_size = "20px",text_slide_number_color = "#d50006",text_slide_number_font_size = "0.5em")
```

```{r xaringanExtra, echo=FALSE}
xaringanExtra::use_progress_bar(color = "#d50006", location = "bottom")
xaringanExtra::use_xaringan_extra(c("tile_view","scribble","panelset","tachyons"))
xaringanExtra::style_panelset_tabs(font_family = "inherit")
#xaringanExtra::use_search(show_icon = TRUE)
#weitere: "share_again","animate_css", "webcam","freezeframe","clipboard","fit_screen","extra-styles" 
xaringanExtra::use_editable(expires = 1)
xaringanExtra::use_freezeframe(trigger = "hover")
``` 

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
options(htmltools.dir.version = FALSE)
library(latex2exp)
library(fontawesome)
#library(emo)
source(xfun::from_root("lit","helper.R"))
library(RefManageR)
BibOptions(check.entries = FALSE, 
           bib.style = "authoryear", 
           style = "markdown",
           dashed = TRUE)
bib <- ReadBib(xfun::from_root("lit","my_bib.bib"))
## Fix: Zitiere() wrapper — adapted for English; "and" used as standard
Zitiere <- function(bib, key, ...) {
  gsub(" and ", " und ", Citet(bib, key, ...))
}
```

# ER114 - Data Science and Strategy for Business

## Session 1

### Data Science as Model-Based Abstraction


 

<br>
<br>
<br>
<br>
<br>
<br>
<br>
### FS 2026
<br>
### Prof. Dr. Jörg Schoder
.mycontacts[
`r fa('github')` @FFHS-EconomicResearch
`r fa('linkedin')` @jfschoder
]


---
layout: true

<div class="my-footer"></div>       

<div style="position: absolute;left:400px;bottom:10px;font-size:9px">`r fa('creative-commons')``r rmarkdown::metadata$author`</div>



---
class: left

.blockquote[Agenda]

## Data Science as Model-Based Abstraction

<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:80px 1fr; gap:0.3em 1em;
            font-size:0.82em; margin-top:0.5em; align-items:center;">

<div style="color:#686868;">10'</div>
<div>`r fa('handshake-simple')` <strong>Introductions & Organisational Matters</strong> </div>

<div style="color:#686868;">30'</div>
<div>`r fa('globe')` <strong>Big Picture & Data Strategy</strong> — Walmart scenario · data types & AI · AI User vs. AI Value Creator · translator role · Statistics vs. Data Science </div>

<div style="color:#686868;">20'</div>
<div>`r fa('cogs')` <strong>CRISP-DM & `r fa('r-project')`-Tooling</strong> — </div>


<div style="color:#D50006; font-weight:bold;">40'</div>
<div style="color:#D50006; font-weight:bold;">`r fa('flask')` <strong>Lab 1</strong> — `r fa('r-project')`-projects · data import · tidyverse · Quarto </div>


<div style="color:#686868; font-style:italic;">10'</div>
<div style="color:#686868; font-style:italic;">`r fa('mug-hot')` Pause</div>


<div style="color:#D50006; font-weight:bold;">30'</div>
<div style="color:#D50006; font-weight:bold;">`r fa('flask')` <strong>Lab 2</strong> — Data Understanding & EDA</div>


<div style="color:#D50006; font-weight:bold;">20'</div>
<div style="color:#D50006; font-weight:bold;">`r fa('flask')` <strong>Lab 3</strong> — Modelling · Baseline · Linear Regression</div>


<div style="color:#686868;">10'</div>
<div>`r fa('chart-line')` <strong>Modelling & Demo</strong> — Descriptive Regression (Population) · Complexity · Demo: Multiple Model</div>


<div style="color:#D50006; font-weight:bold;">30'</div>
<div style="color:#D50006; font-weight:bold;">`r fa('flask')` <strong>Lab 4</strong> — Performance Mining</div>

<div style="color:#686868;">10'</div>
<div>`r fa('flag-checkered')` <strong>Debrief & Outlook</strong></div>

</div>


???
* ToDo: Red thread: Carseats dataset through all labs (consistent dataset)
* Goal today: from business problem to first descriptive model (AI User)
* Regression theory deepened in labs — here only descriptive as population


---
class: inverse, center, middle

## Part 1: Introductions & Organisation


---
class: left

.blockquote[Introductions & Organisation]

## Brief Introductions

.panelset[
.panel[.panel-name[Who am I?]

```{r map}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| fig-align: "center"
#| out-height: "400px"
library(leaflet)
m <- leaflet() %>%
  addTiles() %>%  # Add default OpenStreetMap map tiles
  addMarkers(lng=9.326888, lat=48.737254,
    popup="Birth") %>%
  addMarkers(lng=9.01992, lat=48.688465,
    popup="High School") %>%
  addMarkers(lng=10.676478, lat=47.570924,
    popup="Military Service") %>%
  addMarkers(lng=-71.105495, lat=42.3507532,
    popup="Visiting Scholar")  %>%
  addMarkers(lng=7.848034412832279, lat=47.995786921757244,
    popup="PhD") %>%
  addMarkers(lng=13.060978531837467, lat=47.789096688734794,
    popup="Postdoc") %>%
  addMarkers(lng=7.8437536, lat=47.9901194,
    popup="Professor") %>%
  addMarkers(lng=7.846853, lat=47.994721,
    popup="Studies") 
m 
```
]
.panel[.panel-name[Who are you?]
Please briefly introduce yourself in the group, touching on:

* My name is...
* I work in the field / at the company...
* If I weren't here today, I would be...
* My background in quantitative research is...
* My expectations for this session are...
]
]


---
class: left

.blockquote[Introductions & Organisation]

## CHECK

.pull-left[
<br>

.large[.red[**C**]heck your audio settings!]

.large[.red[**H**]ush background noise!]

.large[.red[**E**]ngage & participate!]

.large[.red[**C**]hat is faster than speaking!]

.large[.red[**K**]eep your camera on!]
]

.pull-right[
<svg viewBox="0 0 280 300" width="100%">
  <defs><style>
    .ico { fill: #D50006; }
  </style></defs>

  <!-- Lautsprecher -->
  <g transform="translate(30,10)">
    <rect class="ico" x="0" y="18" width="22" height="28" rx="2"/>
    <polygon class="ico" points="22,8 22,54 46,62 46,0"/>
    <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:16px;font-weight:bold;fill:#D50006" x="52" y="14">+</text>
    <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:16px;font-weight:bold;fill:#D50006" x="52" y="56">−</text>
  </g>

  <!-- Schallwelle -->
  <g transform="translate(105,18)">
    <path d="M0,22 C6,5 10,5 14,22 C18,39 22,39 26,22
             C30,5 34,5 38,22 C42,39 46,39 50,22
             C54,5 58,5 62,22 C66,39 70,39 74,22
             C78,5 82,5 86,22 C90,39 94,39 98,22"
          stroke="#D50006" stroke-width="3"
          fill="none" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Hand / Winken -->
  <g transform="translate(185,0)">
    <rect class="ico" x="25" y="110" width="30" height="65" rx="8"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="15" y="55" width="50" height="65" rx="12"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="0" y="70" width="18" height="32" rx="9"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="16" y="30" width="14" height="38" rx="7"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="32" y="22" width="14" height="38" rx="7"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="48" y="28" width="14" height="36" rx="7"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="53" y="45" width="13" height="30" rx="6"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Handy -->
  <g transform="translate(165,170)">
    <rect class="ico" x="10" y="0" width="55" height="88" rx="8"/>
    <rect x="16" y="8" width="43" height="64" rx="3" fill="white"/>
    <circle class="ico" cx="37" cy="80" r="4"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="24" y="20" width="26" height="16" rx="2"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="24" y="42" width="12" height="12" rx="2"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="40" y="42" width="10" height="12" rx="2"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Kamera -->
  <g transform="translate(80,220)">
    <rect class="ico" x="0" y="20" width="120" height="72" rx="8"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="30" y="8" width="38" height="18" rx="4"/>
    <circle fill="white"   cx="60" cy="58" r="26"/>
    <circle class="ico"    cx="60" cy="58" r="20"/>
    <circle fill="white"   cx="60" cy="58" r="13"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="8" y="28" width="14" height="10" rx="2"/>
    <rect class="ico" x="90" y="10" width="16" height="10" rx="3"/>
  </g>

</svg>
]

---
class: left

.blockquote[Introductions & Organisation]

## Course Concept and Assessment

* Learning objectives according to the [Syllabus](https://moodle.ffhs.ch/mod/url/view.php?id=5215522)

    * Strategic Process & Team Competence
    * Methodological-Statistical Competence

--

* Self-study on Moodle is essential for productive in-person sessions

--

* Assessment:

    * Follow-up assignment (1 of 3 in group work, 35% of final grade)
    * Final project (individual, 65%)



???

* Strategic Process & Team Competence:
  * Students can structure data-driven projects along the CRISP-DM cycle and classify data as a strategic asset for value creation.
  * Students are able to act as an interface between business units and data science teams, realistically estimating effort and leading interdisciplinary teams.
* Methodological-Statistical Competence:
  * Students can efficiently process raw data in R (tidyverse) and identify relevant patterns for business decisions through Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA).
  * Students can interpret statistical models (regression) as model-based abstractions of reality and derive practical implications.
  * Students can quantify estimation uncertainty and weigh the risks of wrong decisions (Type I & II errors) against costs in a business context.


---
class: inverse, center, middle

## Part 2: Big Picture & Data Strategy



---
class: left

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Data-Analytic Thinking: The Walmart Scenario

.panelset[
.panel[.panel-name[The Question]

.pull-left[
### Scenario
Hurricane Frances approaches the US coast, 2004.

Walmart managers in Bentonville, Arkansas query their data:

> **"What do people in coastal regions typically buy in the days before a hurricane?"**

]

.pull-right[
<br><br>
<svg viewBox="0 0 220 200" width="100%" height="180">
  <defs>
    <radialGradient id="hurr" cx="50%" cy="50%">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#502479" stop-opacity="0.8"/>
      <stop offset="60%"  stop-color="#D50006" stop-opacity="0.5"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#D50006" stop-opacity="0.1"/>
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>
  <circle cx="110" cy="95" r="85" fill="url(#hurr)"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:28px;text-anchor:middle;fill:white;font-weight:bold" x="110" y="105">🌀</text>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:11px;text-anchor:middle;fill:white;font-weight:bold" x="110" y="130">Hurricane Frances</text>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9px;text-anchor:middle;fill:rgba(255,255,255,0.8)" x="110" y="145">Florida, 2004</text>
</svg>
]
.center[`r fa('circle-right')` What would **you** expect?]
]

.panel[.panel-name[The Answer]

.pull-left[

### The Surprising Answer from the Data:

**Top-selling items**`r fa('star')`:

**#1:** Strawberry Pop-Tarts 

**#2:** Beer

.small[
> *"Walmart's analysts found that sales of Pop-Tarts, particularly strawberry, increased by 7x before a hurricane. The top-selling item before a storm was beer."*
> .tr[(`r Citet(bib, "provost_data_2013")`, S. 3)]
]
]

.pull-right[

**What do we learn from this?**

`r fa('circle-right')` Data provides **counter-intuitive**, yet profitable insights

`r fa('circle-right')` Intuition ≠ Evidence

`r fa('circle-right')` **Data-Analytic Thinking** as a competence: What questions can I answer with data?

`r fa('circle-right')` Result: Walmart strategically placed Pop-Tarts at store entrances before every hurricane

]
]
]

???

* In 2004, Walmart already had a data warehouse with billions of transactions
* This is an example of "Data-Analytic Thinking": systematically questioning assumptions with data
* Provost & Fawcett: "The goal is to extract useful knowledge from data to solve business problems"
.quelle[`r Citet(bib, "provost_data_2013")`, Kap. 1]



---
class: left

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Data Science: The Walmart Example


.center[.small[`r fa('circle-right')` *For the Pop-Tart insight, Walmart needed exactly this:*
**Organize transaction data** (Computer Science) · **Statistically validate patterns** (Statistics) · **Understand business relevance** (Domain Knowledge)]]

<svg viewBox="0 0 760 300" width="100%" height="280">
  <defs>
    <style>
      .pillar-1 { fill: #D50006; }
      .pillar-2 { fill: #502479; }
      .pillar-3 { fill: #7d0a52; }
      .title    { font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: bold;
                  fill: #ffffff; text-anchor: middle; }
      .item     { font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 11.5px; fill: #1a1a1a;
                  text-anchor: middle; }
      .base     { fill: #686868; }
      .base-lbl { font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: bold;
                  fill: #ffffff; text-anchor: middle; }
    </style>
    <linearGradient id="grad-top" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="0%">
      <stop offset="0%"   stop-color="#D50006"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#502479"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- Dach mit Verlauf -->
  <rect x="30" y="52" width="700" height="20" rx="3" fill="url(#grad-top)"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif; font-size:12px; font-weight:bold;
               fill:#ffffff; text-anchor:middle;"
        x="380" y="67">Methods · Technology · Application</text>

  <!-- ===== Säule 1: Statistik ===== -->
  <rect class="pillar-1" x="45"  y="72" width="200" height="22" rx="3"/>
  <rect class="pillar-1" x="55"  y="94" width="180" height="161" rx="3"/>
  <text class="title" x="145" y="89">Statistics</text>
  <text class="item"  x="145" y="123">Modelling</text>
  <text class="item"  x="145" y="151">Probability</text>
  <text class="item"  x="145" y="179">Inference</text>
  <text class="item"  x="145" y="207">Uncertainty</text>

  <!-- ===== Säule 2: Informatik ===== -->
  <rect class="pillar-2" x="280" y="72" width="200" height="22" rx="3"/>
  <rect class="pillar-2" x="290" y="94" width="180" height="161" rx="3"/>
  <text class="title" x="380" y="89">Computer Science</text>
  <text class="item"  x="380" y="123">Databases / Architecture</text>
  <text class="item"  x="380" y="151">Cloud / Infrastructure</text>
  <text class="item"  x="380" y="179">Data Security</text>
  <text class="item"  x="380" y="207">Programming / Algorithms</text>

  <!-- ===== Säule 3: Domänenwissen ===== -->
  <rect class="pillar-3" x="515" y="72" width="200" height="22" rx="3"/>
  <rect class="pillar-3" x="525" y="94" width="180" height="161" rx="3"/>
  <text class="title" x="615" y="89">Domain Knowledge</text>
  <text class="item"  x="615" y="123">Problem Understanding</text>
  <text class="item"  x="615" y="151">Industry Knowledge</text>
  <text class="item"  x="615" y="179">Interpretation</text>
  <text class="item"  x="615" y="207">Communication</text>

  <!-- Basis -->
  <rect class="base" x="30" y="255" width="700" height="35" rx="4"/>
  <text class="base-lbl" x="380" y="278">Data Science</text>

</svg>




???
* Our focus will be on exploration and modelling
* ER015 Storytelling: Communication



---
class: left

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Data Science and Future Skills for Managers


.panelset[
.panel[.panel-name[Data Science]
.pull-left[
<h3 style="margin-bottom:0;">Cross-disciplinary field</h3>
<svg viewBox="-280 -180 560 370" width="100%" height="320" style="margin-top:-30px; display:block;">  <defs><style>
    .ring { fill: #D50006; fill-opacity: 0.22; stroke: #D50006; stroke-width: 2; }
    .lbl  { font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: bold; fill: #1a1a1a; text-anchor: middle; }
    .ds   { font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 22px; font-weight: bold; fill: #D50006; text-anchor: middle; }
  </style></defs>
  <circle class="ring" cx="0"   cy="-60" r="110"/>
  <circle class="ring" cx="-80" cy="70"  r="110"/>
  <circle class="ring" cx="80"  cy="70"  r="110"/>
  <text class="lbl" x="0"    y="-115">Computer</text>
  <text class="lbl" x="0"    y="-100">Science</text>
  <text class="lbl" x="-145" y="105">Statistics</text>
  <text class="lbl" x="115"  y="105">Domain Knowledge</text>
  <text class="lbl" x="120"  y="120">(Business)</text>
  <text class="ds"  x="0"    y="35">DS</text>
</svg>
]
.pull-right[
### What Data Scientists Do

| Activity | Time Share |
|----------|------------|
| Data Cleaning | 40–60% |
| Feature Engineering | 10–15% |
| Exploration & EDA | 15–20% |
| Modelling | 10–15% |
| Communication | 10–15% |
]

<div style="margin-top:-150px;">

<strong>`r fa('exclamation-circle')` Data Science is more than programming – it requires statistical and domain expertise</strong>

</div>
]
.panel[.panel-name[Future Skills]
* Practical challenge: Data Science (DS) dominated by computer scientists  
  `r fa('circle-right')` Risk of developing solutions that miss business needs

* Managers have domain knowledge but, in a world where data is increasingly strategic, also need:
  * Statistical competence
  * Basic coding skills `r fa('circle-right')` Harvard Business School considers coding essential
      * Large companies: "translation" competence (management ↔ data teams)
      * SMEs: prototyping competence

`r fa('exclamation-circle')` Our guiding idea: build and strengthen competences for the **translator** role
]
]

???

Definition: Data Science is the model-based abstraction of reality through:

* Statistische Methoden
* Informatik-Werkzeuge
* Domaenen-Expertise

* Früher: `r fa('code')` Code schreiben = Kernkompetenz

* Heute `r fa('wand-magic-sparkles')` Generative KI **demokratisiert Coding** und damit Prototyping.

* Konsequenz für dieses Modul
  * Programmierung nicht als Selbstzweck
  * Sondern: Als "Translator" zwischen Fachbereich und Data-Science-Teams agieren
  * Im Zentrum stehen **Validierung, Interpretation und strategische Einordnung**
  * Verifizierung, Validierung und strategische Einordnung sind die neuen Kernkompetenzen


---
class: left

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Business Data Science

* ...as the combination of **data-driven analysis** with **strategic thinking** to make better,
long-term effective decisions.

* It is not just about analysing data (Data Science), but about...
* ...**asking the right questions**,
translating insights into actionable recommendations, and placing these in the broader context of goals, competition,
and future direction (Strategy).

`r fa('exclamation-circle')` Data Science without strategy is analysis without impact —
Strategy without data is opinion at risk.

???

* "Data Science without strategy is analysis without impact."
  * Data creates no value per se (Provost & Fawcett (2013) and Thomas et al. (2025))
  * The "use case" as a success factor
  
* "Strategy without data is opinion at risk"
  * Intuition (opinion) vs. Evidence (data)




---
class: left

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## A Brief History of AI: From Machine Learning to Agentic AI

<svg viewBox="0 0 860 210" width="100%">
  <defs><style>
    .ax { stroke:#333; stroke-width:1.5; }
    .lb { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:9px; fill:#333; text-anchor:middle; }
    .li { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:8px; fill:#686868; text-anchor:middle; font-style:italic; }
    .ct { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:9px; font-weight:bold; text-anchor:middle; }
    .ds { stroke-dasharray:3,2; stroke-width:0.8; }
    .wi { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:8px; fill:#7ab3e0; text-anchor:middle; font-weight:bold; }
    .ws { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:7px; fill:#7ab3e0; text-anchor:middle; }
  </style></defs>
  <!-- Achse -->
  <line class="ax" x1="30" y1="118" x2="845" y2="118"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8px;fill:#333" x="848" y="121">t</text>
  <!-- Zeitmarken -->
  <line x1="80"  y1="114" x2="80"  y2="122" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text class="lb" x="80"  y="132">1950</text>
  <line x1="205" y1="114" x2="205" y2="122" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text class="lb" x="205" y="132">1970</text>
  <line x1="330" y1="114" x2="330" y2="122" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text class="lb" x="330" y="132">1985</text>
  <line x1="453" y1="114" x2="453" y2="122" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text class="lb" x="453" y="132">1995</text>
  <line x1="536" y1="114" x2="536" y2="122" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text class="lb" x="536" y="132">2000</text>
  <line x1="703" y1="114" x2="703" y2="122" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text class="lb" x="703" y="132">2017</text>
  <line x1="786" y1="114" x2="786" y2="122" stroke="#333" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text class="lb" x="786" y="132">2022</text>
  <!-- Phasen-Bänder -->
  <rect x="80"  y="110" width="166" height="8" rx="0" fill="#b3b2b2" fill-opacity="0.4"/>
  <text class="ct" x="163" y="104" fill="#b3b2b2">Symbolic AI</text>
  <rect x="246" y="118" width="83" height="8" rx="2" fill="#7ab3e0" fill-opacity="0.8"/>
  <text class="wi" x="287" y="180">❄ 1st AI Winter</text>
  <text class="ws" x="287" y="191">~1974–1980</text>
  <rect x="412" y="118" width="83" height="8" rx="2" fill="#7ab3e0" fill-opacity="0.8"/>
  <text class="wi" x="453" y="180">❄ 2nd AI Winter</text>
  <text class="ws" x="453" y="191">~1987–1993</text>
  <!-- Alle AI-Labels -->
  <text class="lb" x="122" y="52">Multivariate Statistics,</text>
  <text class="li" x="122" y="65">Perceptron, Backprop.</text>
  <line class="ds" x1="122" y1="69" x2="122" y2="111" stroke="#aaa"/>
  <text class="lb" x="380" y="52">Expert Systems</text>
  <text class="li" x="380" y="65">(rule-based)</text>
  <line class="ds" x1="380" y1="69" x2="380" y2="111" stroke="#aaa"/>

  <rect x="495" y="110" width="208" height="8" rx="0" fill="#7d0a52" fill-opacity="0.2"/>
  <text class="ct" x="560" y="38" fill="#7d0a52">Predictive AI</text>
  <text class="lb" x="560" y="54">Machine Learning,</text>
  <text class="lb" x="560" y="68">Deep Learning</text>
  <line class="ds" x1="536" y1="72" x2="536" y2="110" stroke="#7d0a52" stroke-width="1.5"/>

  <rect x="703" y="110" width="83" height="8" rx="0" fill="#D50006" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text class="ct" x="730" y="38" fill="#D50006">Generative AI</text>
  <text class="lb" x="730" y="54">Foundation Models,</text>
  <text class="lb" x="730" y="68">Transformer, GPT…</text>
  <line class="ds" x1="703" y1="72" x2="703" y2="110" stroke="#D50006" stroke-width="1.5"/>

  <!-- Agentic AI NEU -->
  <rect x="786" y="110" width="60" height="8" rx="0" fill="#502479" fill-opacity="0.28"/>
  <text class="ct" x="820" y="38" fill="#502479">Agentic AI</text>
  <text class="lb" x="820" y="54">autonomous,</text>
  <text class="lb" x="820" y="68">multi-step</text>
  <line class="ds" x1="800" y1="72" x2="800" y2="110" stroke="#502479" stroke-width="1.5"/>
</svg>

<!-- Treiber-Box direkt unter SVG, kein Whitespace -->
.small[
<div style="border:1px solid #b3b2b2; border-radius:5px;
            padding:5px 14px; background:#f9f9f9; margin-top:0.3em;">
<strong>Why the breakthrough from 2000?</strong> &emsp;
`r fa('microchip')` Computing power &nbsp;·&nbsp;
`r fa('database')` Large datasets &nbsp;·&nbsp;
`r fa('cloud')` Open-source &amp; Cloud &nbsp;·&nbsp;
`r fa('arrow-trend-up')` Critical mass
&emsp;<span style="color:#D50006; font-weight:bold;">→ self-reinforcing effects</span>
</div>
]

???
* AI Winter: inflated expectations → funding cuts
* Transformer 2017: Vaswani et al. "Attention is all you need"
* For ER014: we position ourselves in the Predictive AI area (ML/Regression)



---
class: left

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## The transformative potential of generative AI lies in the fact that...

...for the first time, different data structures can be processed seamlessly!


<style>
.dt-grid {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: row;
  gap: 0.8em;
  margin-top: 0.3em;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.dt-col {
  flex: 1;
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
  position: relative;
}
.dt-hdr {
  color: white;
  font-family: sans-serif;
  font-size: 12px;
  font-weight: bold;
  padding: 6px 12px;
  border-radius: 5px 5px 0 0;
  flex-shrink: 0;
}
.dt-body {
  border-top: none;
  border-radius: 0 0 5px 5px;
  padding: 10px 12px;
  font-size: 0.79em;
  flex: 1;
  box-sizing: border-box;
}
.dt-chip {
  background: #f0f0f0;
  border-radius: 4px;
  padding: 3px 7px;
  font-size: 0.88em;
  display: inline-block;
  margin: 2px 2px;
}
.dt-hint {
  padding: 6px 9px;
  font-size: 0.84em;
  line-height: 1.5;
  border-radius: 0 3px 3px 0;
  margin-top: 8px;
}
.dt-synth {
  margin-top: 0.5em;
  border: 1px solid #b3b2b2;
  border-radius: 5px;
  padding: 6px 14px;
  background: #fafafa;
  font-size: 0.79em;
  line-height: 1.6;
}
/* ── ER-Badges: untere rechte Ecke, minimal überlappend ── */
.er-badge {
  position: absolute;
  color: white;
  font-family: sans-serif;
  font-size: 11px;
  font-weight: bold;
  padding: 6px 12px;
  border-radius: 5px;
  border: 2px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.55);
  box-shadow: 0 3px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.28);
  transform: rotate(40deg);
  opacity: 0;
  z-index: 10;
}
.er-018 {
  background: rgba(80, 36, 121, 0.90);
  bottom: 49px;
  right: -13px;
  animation: popIn 0.45s ease-out 1.0s forwards;
}
.er-016 {
  background: rgba(213, 0, 6, 0.88);
  bottom: 5px;
  right: 9px;
  animation: popIn 0.45s ease-out 1.7s forwards;
}
@keyframes popIn {
  from { opacity: 0; transform: rotate(40deg) scale(0.65); }
  to   { opacity: 1; transform: rotate(40deg) scale(1); }
}
</style>

<div class="dt-grid">

<!-- ── Strukturiert ── -->
<div class="dt-col">
  <div class="dt-hdr" style="background:#7d0a52;">
    📊 Structured Data
  </div>
  <div class="dt-body" style="border:2px solid #7d0a52;">
    <strong>Tabular · fixed schema · directly analysable</strong>
    <div style="margin:8px 0;">
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('file-excel', fill='#217346')` Excel / CSV</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('database', fill='#336791')` SQL</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('table', fill='#686868')` ERP</span>
    </div>
    <table style="font-size:0.9em; border-collapse:collapse; width:100%; margin:4px 0 8px 0;">
      <tr style="background:#f9f0f3;">
        <th style="padding:3px 6px; text-align:left; border:1px solid #e0c8d0;">Branch</th>
        <th style="padding:3px 6px; border:1px solid #e0c8d0;">Revenue</th>
        <th style="padding:3px 6px; border:1px solid #e0c8d0;">Price</th>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">London</td>
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; text-align:center; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">12.4</td>
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; text-align:center; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">89</td>
      </tr>
      <tr style="background:#fdf6f8;">
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">Manchester</td>
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; text-align:center; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">8.1</td>
        <td style="padding:3px 6px; text-align:center; border:1px solid #ebebeb;">95</td>
      </tr>
    </table>
    <div class="dt-hint" style="background:#f9f0f3; border-left:3px solid #7d0a52;">
      `r fa('circle-right')` Taddy (2019): <strong>"flat file"</strong><br>
      1 row = 1 observation<br>
      1 column = 1 feature<br>
      → directly usable for ML
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- ── Unstrukturiert + ER-Badges ── -->
<div class="dt-col">
  <div class="dt-hdr" style="background:#D50006;">
    🎵 Unstructured Data
  </div>
  <div class="dt-body" style="border:2px solid #D50006;">
    <strong>No schema · ~80% of all corporate data</strong>
    <div style="margin:8px 0;">
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('file-word', fill='#2b579a')` Word</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('file-pdf', fill='#e74c3c')` PDF</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('file-lines', fill='#555')` E-Mail / Text</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('music', fill='#e74c3c')` MP3</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('video', fill='#8e44ad')` MP4</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('image', fill='#16a085')` JPG / PNG</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('comment', fill='#686868')` Social Media</span>
    </div>
    <div class="dt-hint" style="background:#fff0f0; border-left:3px solid #D50006;">
      `r fa('circle-right')` Long difficult to use —<br>
      only <strong>Foundation Models</strong> (GPT etc.)<br>
      unlock this type<br>
      at scale
    </div>
  </div>
  <div class="er-badge er-018">ER018</div>
  <div class="er-badge er-016">ER016</div>
</div>

<!-- ── Semi-strukturiert ── -->
<div class="dt-col">
  <div class="dt-hdr" style="background:#502479;">
    🔗 Semi-structured Data
  </div>
  <div class="dt-body" style="border:2px solid #502479;">
    <strong>Flexible schema · nested</strong>
    <div style="margin:8px 0;">
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('code', fill='#e67e22')` JSON</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('file-code', fill='#2980b9')` XML</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('globe', fill='#27ae60')` HTML</span>
      <span class="dt-chip">`r fa('envelope', fill='#686868')` E-Mail-Header</span>
    </div>
    <div style="font-size:0.82em; background:#f4f4f4; border-radius:4px;
                padding:5px 8px; margin:6px 0; font-family:monospace;
                line-height:1.4; color:#333;">
      {"branch": "London",<br>
      &nbsp;"umsatz": 12.4,<br>
      &nbsp;"prod": [{"id":1,"p":89}]}
    </div>
    <div class="dt-hint" style="background:#f5f0fa; border-left:3px solid #502479;">
      `r fa('circle-right')` Taddy: <strong>"nested data"</strong><br>
      Rel. DB: normalised tables<br>
      For ML: first <strong>flatten</strong>!<br>
      (denormalise)
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

</div><!-- dt-grid -->

<div class="dt-synth">
`r fa('circle-right')` <strong>Taddy (2019):</strong>
ML algorithms almost always expect a <strong>flat file</strong> —
unstructured and nested data must first be processed
(`r fa('circle-right')` Feature Engineering).
</div>

???
* nested → flat: often the most expensive step in data engineering
* In this module we primarily use **structured data** and **flat data only**
* ER018 / ER016: modules dealing with unstructured data





---
class: left

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## AI User vs. AI Value Creator

.panelset[
.panel[.panel-name[The Concept]

<svg viewBox="0 0 680 200" width="90%" style="display:block;margin:auto">
  <defs>
    <style>
      .rtxt { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:11px; font-weight:bold;
              fill:white; text-anchor:middle; }
      .rarr { stroke:#686868; stroke-width:2; fill:none;
              marker-end:url(#arr); }
      .anno { font-family:sans-serif; font-size:10px; fill:#686868;
              text-anchor:middle; }
    </style>
    <marker id="arr" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8"
            refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L0,6 L8,3 z" fill="#686868"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <!-- Stufen der AI Ladder -->
  <rect x="10"  y="140" width="130" height="40" rx="6" fill="#b3b2b2"/>
  <text class="rtxt" x="75"  y="165">1. Collect</text>

  <rect x="180" y="140" width="130" height="40" rx="6" fill="#686868"/>
  <text class="rtxt" x="245" y="165">2. Organise</text>

  <rect x="350" y="140" width="130" height="40" rx="6" fill="#7d0a52"/>
  <text class="rtxt" x="415" y="165">3. Analyse</text>

  <rect x="520" y="140" width="140" height="40" rx="6" fill="#502479"/>
  <text class="rtxt" x="590" y="165">4. Infuse</text>

  <!-- Pfeile -->
  <line class="rarr" x1="145" y1="160" x2="175" y2="160"/>
  <line class="rarr" x1="315" y1="160" x2="345" y2="160"/>
  <line class="rarr" x1="485" y1="160" x2="515" y2="160"/>

  <!-- Trennlinie User vs Creator -->
  <line x1="340" y1="20" x2="340" y2="135"
        stroke="#D50006" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5,3"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:10px;fill:#D50006;
               text-anchor:middle;font-weight:bold" x="340" y="15">
    AI User | AI Value Creator
  </text>

  <!-- Annotations -->
  <text class="anno" x="170" y="125">Standard Tools</text>
  <text class="anno" x="170" y="137">Commodity Data</text>
  <text class="anno" x="510" y="125">Proprietary Data</text>
  <text class="anno" x="510" y="137">Sustainable Advantage</text>
</svg>

]

.panel[.panel-name[AI User]

`r fa('user')` **AI User** *(where we start today)*

* Uses **standard models** (ChatGPT, Copilot) and **public data**
* Result: Operational efficiency, faster processes
* **But:** Every competitor can do the same → "Commodity"
* No sustainable competitive advantage

<br>
.blockquote[
"If your competitive advantage depends on a tool that anyone can buy, it is not a competitive advantage."
.tr[`r Citet(bib, "thomasAIValueCreators2025")`]
]
]

.panel[.panel-name[AI Value Creator]

`r fa('rocket')` **AI Value Creator** *(the strategic goal)*

* Uses **Proprietary Data** — own, exclusive datasets
* Trains models that **competitors cannot copy**
* Creates **Data Moats**

<br>

**Examples:**
.small[
* Amazon: purchase history of 300M customers → recommendation algorithm impossible to copy
* Netflix: viewing data → content decisions (House of Cards)
* Walmart 2004: own transaction data → Pop-Tart insight no competitor had
]

.quellePanURL[`r Citet(bib, "thomasAIValueCreators2025")`]
]
]

???

* Today we are AI Users — we use the Carseats dataset (public)
* Strategic goal: understand when building own data infrastructure pays off



---
class: left
.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]
## The Jump from Prediction to Value Creation

.pull-left[
<div style="background:#f8f4ff;border:1px solid #c5b8e8;border-radius:8px;overflow:hidden;font-size:0.72em;height:320px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;">
<div style="background:#502479;color:white;padding:7px 14px;flex-shrink:0;">
<strong>💻 (Pure) Data Science</strong> &nbsp;·&nbsp; <span style="opacity:0.8;font-size:0.9em;">AI User</span>
</div>
<div style="padding:10px 14px;flex:1;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;">
<p style="margin:0"><span style="color:#3C3489;font-weight:bold;">Core task</span><br>
Pattern recognition, prediction, content generation </p>
<p style="margin:0"><span style="color:#3C3489;font-weight:bold;">Data</span><br>
<em>Commodity Data</em> — the public internet </p>
<p style="margin:0"><span style="color:#3C3489;font-weight:bold;">Methodology</span><br>
Off-the-shelf Foundation Models — Black Box </p>
<div style="background:#fdf0f0;border-left:3px solid #D50006;padding:7px 10px;border-radius:0 4px 4px 0;margin-top:auto;">
⚠ <strong style="color:#A32D2D;">Problem:</strong>
<span style="color:#A32D2D;">Only operational efficiency (+AI). Every competitor uses the same tools — no sustainable competitive advantage.</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>
]

.pull-right[
<div style="background:#fff5f5;border:1px solid #e8b8b8;border-radius:8px;overflow:hidden;font-size:0.72em;height:320px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;">
<div style="background:#D50006;color:white;padding:7px 14px;flex-shrink:0;">
<strong>📈 Business Data Science</strong> &nbsp;·&nbsp; <span style="opacity:0.8;font-size:0.9em;">AI Value Creator</span>
</div>
<div style="padding:10px 14px;flex:1;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px;">
<p style="margin:0"><span style="color:#791F1F;font-weight:bold;">Core task</span><br>
Decision-making, causal analysis, agentic workflows </p>
<p style="margin:0"><span style="color:#791F1F;font-weight:bold;">Data</span><br>
<em>Proprietary Data</em> — RAG / Fine-Tuning </p>
<p style="margin:0"><span style="color:#791F1F;font-weight:bold;">Methodology</span><br>
Economic domain knowledge + AI+ mindset </p>
<div style="background:#f0faf5;border-left:3px solid #0F6E56;padding:7px 10px;border-radius:0 4px 4px 0;margin-top:auto;">
✓ <strong style="color:#085041;">Solution:</strong>
<span style="color:#0F6E56;">Irreproducible value through fusion of AI and proprietary business data.</span>
</div>
</div>
</div>
]

<br>
<br>
<p style="text-align:center;font-size:0.7em;margin-top:0.5em;color:#555;">
Big Data + ML + LLMs + Statistics = <em>Data Science</em> &nbsp;|&nbsp;
+ Economics + Proprietary Data = <strong>Business Data Science</strong> 
</p>



---
class: left
count: false

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Statistics vs. Data Science — Two Schools of Thought

<style>
.vs2-hdr {
  color: white; font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: bold;
  padding: 6px 12px; border-radius: 4px; text-align: center;
}
.vs2-row {
  display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
  gap: 0.55em; margin-top: 0.28em;
}
.vs2-dim {
  font-family: sans-serif; font-size: 9px; font-weight: bold;
  text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: #777;
  padding: 2px 8px; background: #f5f5f5; border-radius: 3px;
  grid-column: 1 / -1;
}
.vs2-cell {
  border-radius: 4px; padding: 5px 9px;
  font-size: 0.74em; line-height: 1.42;
}
.vs2-chip {
  background: rgba(0,0,0,0.07); border-radius: 3px;
  padding: 1px 4px; font-size: 0.87em;
  display: inline-block; margin: 1px;
}
</style>

<!-- Header -->
<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:0.55em; margin-top:0.2em;">
  <div class="vs2-hdr" style="background:#502479;">
    `r fa('chart-line', fill='white')` &nbsp;Classical Statistics
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-hdr" style="background:#D50006;">
    `r fa('robot', fill='white')` &nbsp;Data Science / ML
  </div>
</div>

<!-- Dim 1: Erkenntnisinteresse — AKTIV -->
<div class="vs2-row">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('bullseye', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Research Interest</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8;">
    <strong style="color:#502479;">`r fa('magnifying-glass')` Explain</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">Inference</span><span class="vs2-chip">Causality</span><span class="vs2-chip">Hypothesis Testing</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Understand parameters · „Why?"</span>
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8;">
    <strong style="color:#D50006;">`r fa('wand-magic-sparkles')` Predict</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">Prediction</span><span class="vs2-chip">Automation</span><span class="vs2-chip">Correlation</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Maximise accuracy · "What comes next?"</span>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- Dim 2–4: ausgegraut -->
<div class="vs2-row" style="opacity:0.13;">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('chart-area', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Dealing with Uncertainty</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8; min-height:36px;"></div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8; min-height:36px;"></div>
</div>
<div class="vs2-row" style="opacity:0.13;">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('flask', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Methods &amp; Evaluation</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8; min-height:36px;"></div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8; min-height:36px;"></div>
</div>
<div class="vs2-row" style="opacity:0.13;">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('database', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Data</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8; min-height:36px;"></div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8; min-height:36px;"></div>
</div>

???
* Shmueli (2010): canonical reference — explaining needs theory, predicting needs stable patterns
* Statistics: data "in the dock" — effects are assumed null until the contrary is proven

---
class: left
count: false

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Statistics vs. Data Science — Two Schools of Thought

<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:0.55em; margin-top:0.2em;">
  <div class="vs2-hdr" style="background:#502479;">`r fa('chart-line', fill='white')` &nbsp;Classical Statistics</div>
  <div class="vs2-hdr" style="background:#D50006;">`r fa('robot', fill='white')` &nbsp;Data Science / ML</div>
</div>

<div class="vs2-row">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('bullseye', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Research Interest</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8;">
    <strong style="color:#502479;">`r fa('magnifying-glass')` Explain</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">Inference</span><span class="vs2-chip">Causality</span><span class="vs2-chip">Hypothesis Testing</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Understand parameters · „Why?"</span>
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8;">
    <strong style="color:#D50006;">`r fa('wand-magic-sparkles')` Predict</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">Prediction</span><span class="vs2-chip">Automation</span><span class="vs2-chip">Correlation</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Maximise accuracy · "What comes next?"</span>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- Dim 2: Unsicherheit — AKTIV -->
<div class="vs2-row">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('chart-area', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Dealing with Uncertainty</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8;">
    <strong style="color:#502479;">`r fa('signal')` Theoretical</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">CLT</span><span class="vs2-chip">α-level</span><span class="vs2-chip">Type I error</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Normal distribution · strict assumptions</span>
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8;">
    <strong style="color:#D50006;">`r fa('arrows-rotate')` Data-driven</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">Bootstrap</span><span class="vs2-chip">Resampling</span><span class="vs2-chip">FDR</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">No distributional assumptions · False Discovery Rate</span>
  </div>
</div>

<!-- Dim 3–4: ausgegraut -->
<div class="vs2-row" style="opacity:0.13;">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('flask', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Methods &amp; Evaluation</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8; min-height:36px;"></div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8; min-height:36px;"></div>
</div>
<div class="vs2-row" style="opacity:0.13;">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('database', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Data</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8; min-height:36px;"></div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8; min-height:36px;"></div>
</div>

---
class: left
count: false

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Statistics vs. Data Science — Two Schools of Thought

<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:0.55em; margin-top:0.2em;">
  <div class="vs2-hdr" style="background:#502479;">`r fa('chart-line', fill='white')` &nbsp;Classical Statistics</div>
  <div class="vs2-hdr" style="background:#D50006;">`r fa('robot', fill='white')` &nbsp;Data Science / ML</div>
</div>

<div class="vs2-row">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('bullseye', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Research Interest</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8;">
    <strong style="color:#502479;">`r fa('magnifying-glass')` Explain</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">Inference</span><span class="vs2-chip">Causality</span><span class="vs2-chip">Hypothesis Testing</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Understand parameters · „Why?"</span>
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8;">
    <strong style="color:#D50006;">`r fa('wand-magic-sparkles')` Predict</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">Prediction</span><span class="vs2-chip">Automation</span><span class="vs2-chip">Correlation</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Maximise accuracy · "What comes next?"</span>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="vs2-row">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('chart-area', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Dealing with Uncertainty</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8;">
    <strong style="color:#502479;">`r fa('signal')` Theoretical</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">CLT</span><span class="vs2-chip">α-level</span><span class="vs2-chip">Type I error</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Normal distribution · strict assumptions</span>
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8;">
    <strong style="color:#D50006;">`r fa('arrows-rotate')` Data-driven</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">Bootstrap</span><span class="vs2-chip">Resampling</span><span class="vs2-chip">FDR</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">No distributional assumptions · False Discovery Rate</span>
  </div>
</div>
<!-- Dim 3: Methoden — AKTIV -->
<div class="vs2-row">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('flask', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Methods &amp; Evaluation</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8;">
    <strong style="color:#502479;">`r fa('vial')` Parametric</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">t-Test</span><span class="vs2-chip">ANOVA</span><span class="vs2-chip">R²</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Model fit: p-value · Confidence interval (CI)</span>
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8;">
    <strong style="color:#D50006;">`r fa('chart-line')` Predictive</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">Train/Test-Split</span><span class="vs2-chip">Cross-Validation</span><span class="vs2-chip">RMSE</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Model fit: Out-of-sample Error</span>
  </div>
</div>
<!-- Dim 4: ausgegraut -->
<div class="vs2-row" style="opacity:0.13;">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('database', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Data</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8; min-height:36px;"></div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8; min-height:36px;"></div>
</div>

---
class: left
count: false

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Statistics vs. Data Science — Two Schools of Thought

<div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:0.55em; margin-top:0.2em;">
  <div class="vs2-hdr" style="background:#502479;">`r fa('chart-line', fill='white')` &nbsp;Classical Statistics</div>
  <div class="vs2-hdr" style="background:#D50006;">`r fa('robot', fill='white')` &nbsp;Data Science / ML</div>
</div>

<div class="vs2-row">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('bullseye', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Research Interest</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8;">
    <strong style="color:#502479;">`r fa('magnifying-glass')` Explain</strong> · <span class="vs2-chip">Inference</span><span class="vs2-chip">Causality</span>
    <span style="color:#666;display:block;">Understand parameters · „Why?"</span>
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8;">
    <strong style="color:#D50006;">`r fa('wand-magic-sparkles')` Predict</strong> · <span class="vs2-chip">Prädiktion</span><span class="vs2-chip">Correlation</span>
    <span style="color:#666;display:block;">Maximise accuracy · "What comes next?</span>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="vs2-row">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('chart-area', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Dealing with Uncertainty</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8;">
    <strong style="color:#502479;">`r fa('signal')` Theoretical</strong> · <span class="vs2-chip">CLT</span><span class="vs2-chip">α-level</span>
    <span style="color:#666;display:block;">Normal distribution · strict assumptions</span>
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8;">
    <strong style="color:#D50006;">`r fa('arrows-rotate')` Data-driven</strong> · <span class="vs2-chip">Bootstrap</span><span class="vs2-chip">FDR</span>
    <span style="color:#666;display:block;">No distributional assumptions · Resampling</span>
  </div>
</div>
<div class="vs2-row">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('flask', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Methods &amp; Evaluation</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8;">
    <strong style="color:#502479;">`r fa('vial')` Parametric</strong> · <span class="vs2-chip">t-Test</span><span class="vs2-chip">ANOVA</span><span class="vs2-chip">R²</span>
    <span style="color:#666;display:block;">Model fit: p-value · CI</span>
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8;">
    <strong style="color:#D50006;">`r fa('chart-line')` Predictive</strong> · <span class="vs2-chip">Train/Test</span><span class="vs2-chip">CV</span><span class="vs2-chip">RMSE</span>
    <span style="color:#666;display:block;">Out-of-sample Error</span>
  </div>
</div>
<!-- Dim 4: Daten — AKTIV -->
<div class="vs2-row">
  <div class="vs2-dim">`r fa('database', fill='#777')` &nbsp;Data</div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#f5f0fa; border:1px solid #d0b8e8;">
    <strong style="color:#502479;">`r fa('table')` small to medium</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">n &lt;&lt; 1000</span><span class="vs2-chip">few variables</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Theory-based · structured</span>
  </div>
  <div class="vs2-cell" style="background:#fff0f0; border:1px solid #f0b8b8;">
    <strong style="color:#D50006;">`r fa('database')` Big Data (TB to PB)</strong> &nbsp;
    <span class="vs2-chip">n &gt;&gt; 1000</span><span class="vs2-chip">many cariables</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Data Warehouse/Lakes · data-driven</span>
  </div>
</div>

???
* ER014 builds the bridge: both paradigms are complementary
* PVA1: deskriptive Statistik (Population) — PVA2: Inferenz (Stichprobe)
* Big Data/Dimensionalität: kommt im Kontext Modellierung (Overfitting, FDR)


---
class: left

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Einordnung ER014: Bridging Statistics and Data Science

<svg viewBox="0 0 860 195" width="100%">
  <defs>
    <marker id="darr" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="8" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto">
      <path d="M0,0 L0,6 L8,3 z" fill="#b3b2b2"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

  <!-- Bereiche -->
  <rect x="15"  y="18" width="195" height="160" rx="5" fill="#502479" fill-opacity="0.08" stroke="#502479" stroke-width="1.2"/>
  <rect x="645" y="18" width="200" height="160" rx="5" fill="#D50006" fill-opacity="0.08" stroke="#D50006" stroke-width="1.2"/>
  <rect x="210" y="14" width="435" height="168" rx="6" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.10" stroke="#D5A000" stroke-width="1.8"/>

  <!-- Bereichs-Labels -->
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:11.5px;font-weight:bold;fill:#502479;text-anchor:middle"
        x="112" y="14">Traditional Statistics</text>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:11.5px;font-weight:bold;fill:#D5A000;text-anchor:middle"
        x="432" y="10">ER114 — Modern Statistics &amp; Transition</text>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:11.5px;font-weight:bold;fill:#D50006;text-anchor:middle"
        x="755" y="14">Data Science / ML</text>

  <!-- Tags Klassisch -->
  <rect x="22"  y="42" width="80" height="20" rx="3" fill="#502479" fill-opacity="0.15" stroke="#502479" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#502479;text-anchor:middle" x="62" y="56">EDA · Visualization</text>
  <rect x="112" y="42" width="80" height="20" rx="3" fill="#502479" fill-opacity="0.15" stroke="#502479" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#502479;text-anchor:middle" x="152" y="56">OLS · Regression</text>
  <rect x="22"  y="70" width="80" height="20" rx="3" fill="#502479" fill-opacity="0.15" stroke="#502479" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#502479;text-anchor:middle" x="62" y="84">t-test · ANOVA</text>
  <rect x="112" y="70" width="80" height="20" rx="3" fill="#502479" fill-opacity="0.15" stroke="#502479" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#502479;text-anchor:middle" x="152" y="84">p-value · α-level</text>
  <rect x="22"  y="98" width="80" height="20" rx="3" fill="#502479" fill-opacity="0.15" stroke="#502479" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#502479;text-anchor:middle" x="62" y="112">CLT · Normal dist.</text>
  <rect x="112" y="98" width="80" height="20" rx="3" fill="#502479" fill-opacity="0.15" stroke="#502479" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#502479;text-anchor:middle" x="152" y="112">Multiple Regression</text>

  <!-- Tags ER014-Brücke: 3 Blöcke -->
  <rect x="222" y="38" width="132" height="140" rx="5" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.12" stroke="#D5A000" stroke-width="1.2"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9px;font-weight:bold;fill:#6b4f00;text-anchor:middle" x="288" y="54">① EDA &amp; Modelling</text>
  <rect x="230" y="60" width="116" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="288" y="73">EDA vs. Data Mining</text>
  <rect x="230" y="82" width="116" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="288" y="95">Linear Regression</text>
  <rect x="230" y="104" width="116" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="288" y="117">Conditional mean</text>
  <rect x="230" y="126" width="116" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="288" y="139">Population model</text>
  <rect x="230" y="148" width="116" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="288" y="161">Residual analysis</text>

  <rect x="362" y="38" width="132" height="140" rx="5" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.12" stroke="#D5A000" stroke-width="1.2"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9px;font-weight:bold;fill:#6b4f00;text-anchor:middle" x="428" y="54">② Inference (modern)</text>
  <rect x="370" y="60" width="116" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="428" y="73">Only One Test (Resampling)</text>
  <rect x="370" y="82" width="116" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="428" y="95">Bootstrap · infer-library</text>
  <rect x="370" y="104" width="116" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="428" y="117">Type 1/2 Error · FDR</text>
  <rect x="370" y="126" width="116" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="428" y="139">p-Hacking · Effect size</text>
  <rect x="370" y="148" width="116" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="428" y="161">Significance vs. Relevance</text>

  <rect x="502" y="38" width="140" height="140" rx="5" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.12" stroke="#D5A000" stroke-width="1.2"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9px;font-weight:bold;fill:#6b4f00;text-anchor:middle" x="572" y="54">③ Regression &amp; Causality</text>
  <rect x="510" y="60" width="124" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="572" y="73">Multiple Regression</text>
  <rect x="510" y="82" width="124" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="572" y="95">Multicollinearity</text>
  <rect x="510" y="104" width="124" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="572" y="117">Overfitting · Regularization</text>
  <rect x="510" y="126" width="124" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="572" y="139">Lasso · Ridge</text>
  <rect x="510" y="148" width="124" height="18" rx="3" fill="#D5A000" fill-opacity="0.25"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#5a4000;text-anchor:middle" x="572" y="161">Causal Engineering ➝</text>

  <!-- Tags DS -->
  <rect x="668" y="42" width="174" height="20" rx="3" fill="#D50006" fill-opacity="0.12" stroke="#D50006" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#D50006;text-anchor:middle" x="755" y="56">Logist. Reg. · Supervised ML</text>
  <rect x="668" y="70" width="174" height="20" rx="3" fill="#D50006" fill-opacity="0.12" stroke="#D50006" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#D50006;text-anchor:middle" x="755" y="84">Unsupervised ML · Clustering</text>
  <rect x="668" y="98" width="174" height="20" rx="3" fill="#D50006" fill-opacity="0.12" stroke="#D50006" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#D50006;text-anchor:middle" x="755" y="112">Time Series · Panel Data</text>
  <rect x="668" y="126" width="174" height="20" rx="3" fill="#D50006" fill-opacity="0.12" stroke="#D50006" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#D50006;text-anchor:middle" x="755" y="140">Random Forest · Deep Learning</text>
  <rect x="668" y="154" width="174" height="20" rx="3" fill="#D50006" fill-opacity="0.12" stroke="#D50006" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9.5px;fill:#D50006;text-anchor:middle" x="755" y="168">LLM · GenAI · Big Data</text>

  <!-- Pfeil -->
  <line x1="30" y1="185" x2="830" y2="185"
        stroke="#b3b2b2" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="5,3"
        marker-end="url(#darr)"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:8.5px;fill:#aaa;text-anchor:middle"
        x="430" y="195">methodological evolution</text>

  <!-- ER014 Klammer unten -->
  <line x1="215" y1="185" x2="215" y2="190" stroke="#D5A000" stroke-width="2"/>
  <line x1="215" y1="190" x2="650" y2="190" stroke="#D5A000" stroke-width="2"/>
  <line x1="650" y1="185" x2="650" y2="190" stroke="#D5A000" stroke-width="2"/>

  <!-- Folgemodule -->
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9px;fill:#D50006;text-anchor:middle;font-style:italic"
        x="755" y="195">ER117 · ER118</text>

</svg>

???
* Block ①: EDA vs. Data Mining — heute PVA1
* Block ②: Inferenz (Only One Test) — PVA2
* Block ③: Multiple Regression, Regularisierung — PVA3
* ER017: ML · ER018: NLP/LLM


---
class: left
count: false

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Five Statistical Foundations — and Their Transfer to Data Science

<style>
.bridge-wrap { margin-top: -0.7em; }
.bridge-row {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 38px 1fr;
  gap: 0;
  margin-top: 0.08em;
  align-items: stretch;
}
.bridge-left {
  border-radius: 4px 0 0 4px;
  padding: 4px 8px;
  font-size: 0.70em;
  line-height: 1.33;
  background: #f5f0fa;
  border: 1px solid #c9a8e0;
  border-right: none;
}
.bridge-right {
  border-radius: 0 4px 4px 0;
  padding: 4px 8px;
  font-size: 0.70em;
  line-height: 1.33;
  background: #fff0f0;
  border: 1px solid #f0a8a8;
  border-left: none;
}
.bridge-arrow {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: center;
  background: #4a4a4a;
  font-family: sans-serif;
  font-size: 7.5px;
  font-weight: bold;
  color: white;
  text-align: center;
  line-height: 1.25;
  padding: 3px 2px;
  writing-mode: vertical-lr;
  text-orientation: mixed;
  transform: rotate(180deg);
}
.bridge-kw-l { color: #502479; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.03em; }
.bridge-kw-r { color: #D50006; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.03em; }
.bridge-chip-l {
  background: rgba(80,36,121,0.12); border-radius: 3px;
  padding: 1px 4px; font-size: 0.87em;
  display: inline-block; margin: 1px;
}
.bridge-chip-r {
  background: rgba(213,0,6,0.12); border-radius: 3px;
  padding: 1px 4px; font-size: 0.87em;
  display: inline-block; margin: 1px;
}
.bridge-hdr {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 38px 1fr;
  gap: 0;
  margin-bottom: 0.12em;
}
.bridge-hdr-l {
  background: #502479; color: white; font-family: sans-serif;
  font-size: 11px; font-weight: bold; padding: 4px 9px;
  border-radius: 4px 0 0 4px; text-align: center;
}
.bridge-hdr-c { background: #4a4a4a; }
.bridge-hdr-r {
  background: #D50006; color: white; font-family: sans-serif;
  font-size: 11px; font-weight: bold; padding: 4px 9px;
  border-radius: 0 4px 4px 0; text-align: center;
}
.bridge-ghost { opacity: 0.13; }
</style>

<div class="bridge-wrap">
<div class="bridge-hdr">
  <div class="bridge-hdr-l">`r fa('chart-line', fill='white')` &nbsp;Traditional Concept</div>
  <div class="bridge-hdr-c"></div>
  <div class="bridge-hdr-r">`r fa('robot', fill='white')` &nbsp;Data Science Transfer</div>
</div>
<!-- 1: AKTIV -->
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left">
    <span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('equals')` E[Y|X] — Conditional Expected Value</span><br>
    <span class="bridge-chip-l">OLS</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Regression line</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Population model</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">Regression estimates the mean of Y given X</span>
  </div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Market­bench­mark</div>
  <div class="bridge-right">
    <span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('bullseye')` Residual as Arbitrage Opportunity</span><br>
    <span class="bridge-chip-r">ML model</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">Underperformance</span><br>
    <span style="color:#666;">e = y − ŷ &nbsp;→&nbsp; quantify under-/over-performance</span>
  </div>
</div>
<!-- 2–5: ausgegraut -->
<div class="bridge-row bridge-ghost">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('arrows-rotate')` Sampling distribution</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">—</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('shuffle')` Bootstrap · infer</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row bridge-ghost">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('scale-balanced')` Expected value &amp; error types</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">—</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('coins')` Expected Value Framework</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row bridge-ghost">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('filter')` p-value · α-level · multiplicity</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">—</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('triangle-exclamation')` FDR · p-Hacking</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row bridge-ghost">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('code-branch')` Correlation ≠ Causation</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">—</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('flask')` A/B-Test · Causal ML</span></div>
</div>
</div>

???
* Residual as underperformance measure is the core of the Carseats case study
* E[Y|X]: every ML model — however complex — ultimately estimates this expected value

---
class: left
count: false

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Five Statistical Foundations — and Their Transfer to Data Science

<div class="bridge-wrap">
<div class="bridge-hdr">
  <div class="bridge-hdr-l">`r fa('chart-line', fill='white')` &nbsp;Traditional Concept</div>
  <div class="bridge-hdr-c"></div>
  <div class="bridge-hdr-r">`r fa('robot', fill='white')` &nbsp;Data Science Transfer</div>
</div>
<!-- 1 -->
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('equals')` E[Y|X] — Conditional Expected Value</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">OLS</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Regression line</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Population model</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Regression estimates the mean of Y given X</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Market­bench­mark</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('bullseye')` Residual as Arbitrage Opportunity</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">ML model</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">Underperformance</span><br><span style="color:#666;">e = y − ŷ &nbsp;→&nbsp; quantify under-/over-performance</span></div>
</div>
<!-- 2: AKTIV -->
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('arrows-rotate')` Sampling distribution</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">Standard error</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Confidence interval</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">CLT</span><br><span style="color:#666;">„Idea of repeated sample from population"</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Quantify­uncertainty</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('shuffle')` Bootstrap · infer</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">Resampling</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">no distributional assumption</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Computational power substitutes formulas</span></div>
</div>
<!-- 3–5: ausgegraut -->
<div class="bridge-row bridge-ghost">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('scale-balanced')` Expected value &amp; error types</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">—</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('coins')` Expected Value Framework</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row bridge-ghost">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('filter')` p-value · α-level · multiplicity</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">—</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('triangle-exclamation')` FDR · p-Hacking</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row bridge-ghost">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('code-branch')` Correlation ≠ Causation</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">—</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('flask')` A/B-Test · Causal ML</span></div>
</div>
</div>

---
class: left
count: false

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Five Statistical Foundations — and Their Transfer to Data Science

<div class="bridge-wrap">
<div class="bridge-hdr">
  <div class="bridge-hdr-l">`r fa('chart-line', fill='white')` &nbsp;Traditional Concept</div>
  <div class="bridge-hdr-c"></div>
  <div class="bridge-hdr-r">`r fa('robot', fill='white')` &nbsp;Data Science Transfer</div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('equals')` E[Y|X] — Conditional Expected Value</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">OLS</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Regression line</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Population model</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Regression estimates the mean of Y given X</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Market­bench­mark</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('bullseye')` Residual as Arbitrage Opportunity</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">ML model</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">Underperformance</span><br><span style="color:#666;">e = y − ŷ &nbsp;→&nbsp; quantify under-/over-performance</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('arrows-rotate')` Sampling distribution</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">Standard error</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Confidence interval</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">CLT</span><br><span style="color:#666;">„Idea of repeated sample from population"</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Quantify­uncertainty</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('shuffle')` Bootstrap · infer</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">Resampling</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">no distributional assumption</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Computational power substitutes formula</span></div>
</div>
<!-- 3: AKTIV -->
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('scale-balanced')` Expected value &amp; error types</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">E[X] = Σ pᵢ·xᵢ</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Type I error</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Type II error</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Weigh decisions under uncertainty</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">ROI calculus</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('coins')` Expected Value Framework</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">Model probability</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">Cost/benefit</span><br><span style="color:#666;">"70% churn probability" → financial ROI?</span></div>
</div>
<!-- 4–5: ausgegraut -->
<div class="bridge-row bridge-ghost">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('filter')` p-value · α-level · multiplicity</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">—</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('triangle-exclamation')` FDR · p-Hacking</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row bridge-ghost">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('code-branch')` Correlation ≠ Causation</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">—</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('flask')` A/B-Test · Causal ML</span></div>
</div>
</div>

---
class: left
count: false

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Five Statistical Foundations — and Their Transfer to Data Science

<div class="bridge-wrap">
<div class="bridge-hdr">
  <div class="bridge-hdr-l">`r fa('chart-line', fill='white')` &nbsp;Traditional Concept</div>
  <div class="bridge-hdr-c"></div>
  <div class="bridge-hdr-r">`r fa('robot', fill='white')` &nbsp;Data Science Transfer</div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('equals')` E[Y|X] — Conditional Expected Value</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">OLS</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Regression line</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Regression estimates the mean of Y given X</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Market­bench­mark</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('bullseye')` Residual as Arbitrage Opportunity</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">ML model</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">Underperformance</span><br><span style="color:#666;">e = y − ŷ &nbsp;→&nbsp; quantify under-/over-performance</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('arrows-rotate')` Sampling distribution</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">Standard error</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">CLT</span><br><span style="color:#666;">"Idea of repeated sample from population"</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Measure uncertainty</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('shuffle')` Bootstrap · infer</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">Resampling</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">no distributional assumption</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Computing power replaces formula</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('scale-balanced')` Expected value &amp; error types</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">E[X] = Σ pᵢ·xᵢ</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Type I error</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Type II error</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Weigh decisions under uncertainty</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">ROI calculus</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('coins')` Expected Value Framework</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">Model probability</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">Cost/benefit</span><br><span style="color:#666;">"70% churn probability" → financial ROI?</span></div>
</div>
<!-- 4: AKTIV -->
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('filter')` p-value · α-level · multiplicity</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">α = 0.05</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Hypothesis Testing</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">One test</span><br><span style="color:#666;">For one test: 5% error rate acceptable?</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Signal vs. noise</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('triangle-exclamation')` FDR · p-Hacking</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">1000 tests → 50 false hits</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">Benjamini-Hochberg</span><br><span style="color:#666;">False Discovery Rate controls rate of false alarms</span></div>
</div>
<!-- 5: ausgegraut -->
<div class="bridge-row bridge-ghost">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('code-branch')` Correlation ≠ Causation</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">—</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('flask')` A/B-Test · Causal ML</span></div>
</div>
</div>

---
class: left
count: false

.blockquote[Big Picture & Data Strategy]

## Five Statistical Foundations — and Their Transfer to Data Science

<div class="bridge-wrap">
<div class="bridge-hdr">
  <div class="bridge-hdr-l">`r fa('chart-line', fill='white')` &nbsp;Traditional Concept</div>
  <div class="bridge-hdr-c"></div>
  <div class="bridge-hdr-r">`r fa('robot', fill='white')` &nbsp;Data Science Transfer</div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('equals')` E[Y|X] — Conditional Expected Value</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">OLS</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Regression line</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Regression estimates the mean of Y given X</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Market­bench­mark</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('bullseye')` Residual as Arbitrage Opportunity</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">ML model</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">Underperformance</span><br><span style="color:#666;">e = y − ŷ &nbsp;→&nbsp; quantify under-/over-performance</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('arrows-rotate')` Sampling distribution</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">Standard error</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">CLT</span><br><span style="color:#666;">"Idea of repeated sample from population"</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Measure uncertainty</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('shuffle')` Bootstrap · infer</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">Resampling</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">no distributional assumption</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Computing power replaces formula</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('scale-balanced')` Expected value &amp; error types</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">E[X] = Σ pᵢ·xᵢ</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Type I error</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Type II error</span><br><span style="color:#666;">Decisions under uncertainty</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">ROI-logic</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('coins')` Expected Value Framework</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">Model probability</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">Cost/Benefit</span><br><span style="color:#666;">„70% Churn-probability" → ROI?</span></div>
</div>
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('filter')` p-value · α-level · multiplicity</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">α = 0.05</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Hypothesis Testing</span><br><span style="color:#666;">In a Test: 5% error rate acceptable?</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Signal vs. Noise</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('triangle-exclamation')` FDR · p-Hacking</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">1000 Tests → 50 false findings</span><br><span style="color:#666;">False Discovery Rate controls false alarm</span></div>
</div>
<!-- 5: AKTIV -->
<div class="bridge-row">
  <div class="bridge-left"><span class="bridge-kw-l">`r fa('code-branch')` Correlation ≠ Causation</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-l">Confounder</span><span class="bridge-chip-l">Omitted Variable Bias</span><br><span style="color:#666;">A third variable can create spurious associations</span></div>
  <div class="bridge-arrow">Intervention</div>
  <div class="bridge-right"><span class="bridge-kw-r">`r fa('flask')` A/B-Test · Causal ML</span><br><span class="bridge-chip-r">RCT</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">Conditional Ignorability</span><span class="bridge-chip-r">DAG</span><br><span style="color:#666;">ML fails at interventions — causality is indispensable</span></div>
</div>
</div>

???
* ML predicts well when the future = the past — breaks down with price changes
* All 5 concepts are anchored in ER014 and assumed in ER017/ER018
* Today (Session 1): Concept 1 (E[Y|X]) — Regression as conditional mean




---
class: inverse, center, middle

## Part 3: `r fa('r-project')`-Tooling & CRISP-DM

.blockquote[Retail · Carseats · Problem Translation · R-Tooling]





---
class: left

.blockquote[CRISP-DM & R-Tooling]

## Our Retail Scenario: The Carseats Case Study

.pull-left[
### The Assignment
You are the **Data Science team** of a retail chain:

* `r fa('store')` **400 branches** nationwide
* Main product: **child car seats** (Carseats)
* Problem: Extreme revenue differences between branches

### The Management Question
> *"Find the underperforming branches and evaluate our advertising budget."*

]

.pull-right[
<svg viewBox="0 0 240 220" width="95%">
  <defs><style>
    .pin-good { fill: #502479; }
    .pin-bad  { fill: #D50006; }
    .pin-med  { fill: #7d0a52; }
  </style></defs>
  <!-- Stilisierte Karte -->
  <rect x="10" y="10" width="220" height="180" rx="10"
        fill="#f0f0f0" stroke="#b3b2b2" stroke-width="2"/>
  <!-- Dummy-Umriss Deutschland -->
  <path d="M80,30 Q110,20 140,35 L155,60 Q165,80 158,110
           L145,140 Q130,165 110,170 Q85,175 70,155
           L55,125 Q45,95 55,70 Z"
        fill="#e8e8e8" stroke="#686868" stroke-width="1.5"/>
  <!-- Filial-Pins -->
  <circle class="pin-good" cx="100" cy="65"  r="6"/>
  <circle class="pin-good" cx="130" cy="80"  r="6"/>
  <circle class="pin-med"  cx="115" cy="100" r="6"/>
  <circle class="pin-bad"  cx="90"  cy="120" r="8"/>
  <circle class="pin-good" cx="140" cy="115" r="6"/>
  <circle class="pin-bad"  cx="105" cy="145" r="8"/>
  <circle class="pin-good" cx="125" cy="55"  r="6"/>
  <!-- Legende -->
  <circle class="pin-good" cx="25" cy="168" r="5"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9px;fill:#333" x="34" y="172">Strong branch</text>
  <circle class="pin-bad" cx="25" cy="183" r="5"/>
  <text style="font-family:sans-serif;font-size:9px;fill:#333" x="34" y="187">Underperformer</text>
</svg>


`r fa('circle-right')` Today we start as **AI Users** — public data from `{ISLR2}`

]

???
* Dataset: ISLR2::Carseats — 400 branches, 11 variables
* Sales (thousands of units), Price, Advertising, ShelveLoc, Age, Income, ...
